metadata.title: OfflineRedact — PDF・Word・画像の個人情報自動墨消し
metadata.description: PDF、Word文書、画像から個人データを完全に削除。HIPAA、GDPR、KVKK準拠。100%クライアントサイド処理 — ファイルがブラウザから外に出ることはありません。

hero.badge: 100%クライアントサイド — ファイルがブラウザから外に出ることはありません
hero.title: PDF・文書・画像から個人情報を完全に墨消し
hero.subtitle: PDF、Word文書、画像からPIIと顔を自動墨消し。HIPAA/GDPR/KVKK準拠。
hero.trustedBy: 法律事務所、医療機関、政府機関に信頼されています

features.title: なぜOfflineRedact？
features.subtitle: ブラウザ上で完全に動作するエンタープライズグレードの文書墨消し

trueRedaction.title: 本物の墨消し
trueRedaction.description: 単純な黒塗りとは異なり、PDFから実際のテキストデータを削除します。墨消しされた内容は選択、コピー、復元ができません。

clientSide.title: 100%クライアントサイド
clientSide.description: ファイルがデバイスから外に出ることはありません。すべての処理はブラウザ内で行われます — アップロードなし、サーバーなし、データ漏洩なし。

autoDetect.title: 個人情報の自動検出
autoDetect.description: マイナンバー、氏名、メールアドレス、電話番号、IBAN、クレジットカード番号などを、検証ルール付きのパターンマッチングで自動検出します。

regulations.title: 複数規制への対応
regulations.description: HIPAA、GDPR、KVKK、CCPA、LGPDなどのプロファイルを内蔵。お客様の所在地に基づいて適切なプロファイルを自動選択します。

multiFormat.title: PDF・Word・画像対応
multiFormat.description: PDF、Word文書（.docx）、画像ファイルをすべて同じツールで墨消し。アプリを切り替える必要はありません。

ocrSupport.title: 内蔵OCR
ocrSupport.description: スキャン文書や写真も問題ありません。内蔵OCRが画像からテキストを読み取り、スキャン内の機密データも検出します。

batch.title: 一括処理
batch.description: 複数のファイルを一度にアップロード。すべてを順次処理し、単一のZIPファイルとしてダウンロードできます。

metadata.title: メタデータの削除
metadata.description: 著者、タイトル、作成日、ソフトウェア情報など、機密情報を明かす可能性のある隠れたメタデータを削除します。

howItWorks.title: 使い方
howItWorks.subtitle: 3つの簡単なステップで機密情報を完全に墨消し

step1.title: アップロード
step1.description: PDF、Word文書、画像をドラッグ＆ドロップするか、クリックして選択します。複数ファイルに対応。

step2.title: 確認
step2.description: 自動検出された個人情報がハイライトされます。適用前に墨消しの追加、削除、調整が可能です。

step3.title: ダウンロード
step3.description: 完全に墨消しされたファイルをダウンロード。元のテキストは復元不可能な状態で削除されます。

comparison.title: 他のツールはファイルをアップロードします。私たちはしません。
comparison.subtitle: ほとんどの墨消しツールは、ドキュメント全体をサーバーにアップロードする必要があります。OfflineRedactは異なる方法で動作します。

comparison.footnote: すべての検出はブラウザ内でローカルに実行されます。ファイルがデバイスから出ることはありません。
comparison.dataTypesSubtitle: Automatically finds and redacts sensitive information

security.title: 真の永続的墨消し
security.subtitle: 単に黒いボックスを重ねるだけの基本的なPDFエディタとは異なり、OfflineRedactは機密データなしの新しいドキュメントを作成します。隠されるのではなく、出力に存在しません。
security.noCopyDesc: テキストは出力に含まれない
security.noTricksDesc: 隠されたのではなく、削除された

meta.title: Pricing — OfflineRedact PDF Redaction Tool
meta.description: Start free or upgrade to Pro for $6.99/mo. Remove watermarks, batch process documents, and get priority support.

pricing.guaranteeDesc: Not satisfied? Get a full refund within 30 days, no questions asked.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes. Your files never leave your browser. All processing happens 100% client-side — no uploads, no servers, no data leaks.
faq.a2: Absolutely. We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee, no questions asked. Contact support@offlineredact.com.
faq.a3: PDF documents, Word files (.docx), and images (JPG, PNG, WebP, TIFF). All from the same tool — no need to switch apps.
faq.a4: Yes. Once the page is loaded, the tool works completely offline. Your documents never need an internet connection to be processed.
faq.q5: Can it detect faces in photos?
faq.a5: Yes. OfflineRedact includes built-in face detection that automatically finds and blurs faces in documents and images — a feature unique to OfflineRedact.

meta.title: Privacy Policy — OfflineRedact
meta.description: Learn how OfflineRedact protects your privacy. Your files never leave your browser. Zero data collection, GDPR & CCPA compliant.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.highlight: Your files never leave your browser. All document processing happens 100% on your device — we have zero access to your data.

intro.heading: 1. Introduction
intro.text: OfflineRedact ("we", "our", "us") operates the website offlineredact.com and provides a client-side document redaction tool. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard your information when you visit our website and use our services.
intro.text2: We are committed to protecting your personal data in compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR), the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA/CPRA), and other applicable data protection laws worldwide.
intro.text3: By using OfflineRedact, you consent to the data practices described in this policy. If you do not agree with the terms of this policy, please do not access or use our services.

noFileUploads.heading: 2. Zero-Knowledge Document Processing
noFileUploads.text: This is the core of our privacy commitment: {strong}your documents never leave your device.{/strong}
noFileUploads.text2: All document processing — including text extraction, PII (Personally Identifiable Information) detection, face detection, OCR (Optical Character Recognition), and redaction — happens entirely within your web browser using JavaScript and WebAssembly. No document content is ever transmitted to our servers or any third-party service.
noFileUploads.text3: This means we operate on a zero-knowledge architecture: we cannot see, access, read, store, or process the content of any document you work with. Even if compelled by a court order, we would have no document data to produce.
items: PDF documents are parsed and redacted in-browser using pdf-lib
Word documents (.docx) are processed by extracting and modifying XML in-browser
Images are analyzed for faces using TensorFlow.js running locally in your browser
OCR is performed using Tesseract.js — no cloud OCR services are used
Redacted files are generated locally and downloaded directly to your device

infoCollect.heading: 3. Information We Collect
infoCollect.text: We collect minimal information, categorized as follows:
accountItems: Email address — provided when you create an account or purchase a Pro license
Authentication data — managed securely by Supabase Auth (Google OAuth or magic link)
paymentItems: Payment is processed entirely by Stripe. We never receive, store, or have access to your credit card number, CVV, or billing address
We receive from Stripe: transaction confirmation, subscription status, and customer email
analyticsItems: Page views and feature usage via Google Tag Manager (anonymized, no personal data)
No document content, file names, or PII detected in your documents is ever transmitted
autoItems: IP address — logged temporarily by our hosting provider (Vercel) for security and abuse prevention; not stored by us
Browser type and operating system — collected anonymously via analytics
Referring URL and pages visited — for understanding traffic sources

cookies.heading: 4. Cookies & Local Storage
cookies.text: We use minimal cookies and local storage. We do not use advertising or tracking cookies.

thirdParty.heading: 5. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.text: We use the following third-party services, each with their own privacy policies:

services.name: Google Tag Manager
services.dataShared: Anonymized page view and feature usage data

dataRetention.heading: 6. Data Retention
dataRetention.text: We retain data only as long as necessary for its intended purpose:
items: Account data (email, auth tokens) — retained while your account is active; deleted within 30 days of account deletion request
Payment records — retained for 7 years as required by tax and financial regulations
Analytics data — aggregated and anonymized; individual session data retained for 26 months (Google Analytics default)
Server logs (Vercel) — automatically purged after 30 days
Document content — never stored; exists only in your browser memory during processing

rights.heading: 7. Your Rights
rights.text: Depending on your location, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

gdprRights.title: Right of Access
gdprRights.desc: Request a copy of the personal data we hold about you

gdprRights.title: Right to Rectification
gdprRights.desc: Request correction of inaccurate personal data

gdprRights.title: Right to Erasure
gdprRights.desc: Request deletion of your personal data ("right to be forgotten")

gdprRights.title: Right to Restriction
gdprRights.desc: Request that we limit how we use your data

gdprRights.title: Right to Data Portability
gdprRights.desc: Receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format

gdprRights.title: Right to Object
gdprRights.desc: Object to processing based on legitimate interests

ccpaRights: Right to know what personal information is collected and how it is used
Right to delete personal information held by us
Right to opt-out of the sale of personal information — we do not sell your data
Right to non-discrimination for exercising your privacy rights
rights.exerciseText: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at privacy@offlineredact.com. We will respond within 30 days (GDPR) or 45 days (CCPA).

children.heading: 8. Children's Privacy
children.text: OfflineRedact is not directed at children under the age of 16 (GDPR) or 13 (COPPA). We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us at privacy@offlineredact.com and we will promptly delete such information.

transfers.heading: 9. International Data Transfers
transfers.text: Our hosting provider (Vercel) and payment processor (Stripe) operate servers in the United States and other countries. When your account or payment data is processed by these services, it may be transferred outside your country of residence.
transfers.text2: These transfers are protected by Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission, and by each provider's compliance with applicable data protection frameworks. Your document content is never transferred, as it is processed entirely on your device.

security.heading: 10. Data Security
security.text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal data:
items: Zero-knowledge architecture — document content never leaves your browser
HTTPS/TLS encryption for all website communications
Stripe PCI DSS Level 1 compliance for payment processing
Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) for database access control
Regular security audits and dependency updates
No sensitive data stored on our servers — minimal attack surface

legalBasis.heading: 11. Legal Basis for Processing (GDPR)
legalBasis.text: Under the GDPR, we process personal data based on the following legal grounds:

bases.description: Processing your payment and providing Pro features you purchased
Website analytics to improve our service; security and fraud prevention
Sending marketing communications (only if you opt in)
Retaining payment records as required by tax law

breach.heading: 12. Data Breach Notification
breach.text: In the unlikely event of a data breach affecting your personal data, we will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours as required by GDPR Article 33. If the breach poses a high risk to your rights and freedoms, we will also notify you directly without undue delay.
breach.text2: Given our zero-knowledge architecture, any potential breach would be limited to account and payment metadata — your document content can never be compromised as it is never stored on our servers.

changes.heading: 13. Changes to This Policy
changes.text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, or legal requirements. When we make material changes, we will:
items: Update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page
Display a prominent notice on our website for 30 days
Send an email notification to registered Pro users
Maintain an archive of previous versions upon request

authorities.heading: 14. Regulatory Authorities
authorities.text: If you believe we have not adequately addressed your data protection concerns, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority:
items: EU — Your national Data Protection Authority (DPA)
UK — Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) — ico.org.uk
France — Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés (CNIL) — cnil.fr
Germany — Bundesbeauftragte für den Datenschutz (BfDI) — bfdi.bund.de
Spain — Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) — aepd.es
Turkey — Kişisel Verileri Koruma Kurumu (KVKK) — kvkk.gov.tr
California (US) — Office of the Attorney General — oag.ca.gov

contact.heading: 15. Contact Us
contact.text: For privacy-related inquiries, data subject requests, or complaints:
items: Privacy inquiries: privacy@offlineredact.com
General support: support@offlineredact.com
Legal matters: legal@offlineredact.com
contact.responseTime: We aim to respond to all privacy inquiries within 5 business days.

meta.title: Terms of Service — OfflineRedact
meta.description: Terms and conditions for using the OfflineRedact document redaction tool. 30-day money-back guarantee included.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.highlightGuarantee: 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee — Not satisfied? Get a full refund within 30 days, no questions asked.
terms.highlightOwnership: Your files never leave your device — you retain full ownership of all documents processed with OfflineRedact.

acceptance.heading: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.text: By accessing or using OfflineRedact (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree to all of these Terms, do not access or use the Service.
acceptance.text2: You must be at least 16 years old (or the age of digital consent in your jurisdiction) to use this Service. By using the Service, you represent that you meet this age requirement.
acceptance.text3: If you are using the Service on behalf of an organization, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that organization to these Terms.

serviceDesc.heading: 2. Service Description
serviceDesc.text: OfflineRedact is a client-side document redaction tool that detects and permanently removes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from documents. The Service includes the following capabilities:
features: PDF document redaction — true text removal from PDF content streams
Word document (.docx) redaction — PII removal from document XML
Image redaction — face detection and blurring using local AI models
OCR (Optical Character Recognition) — text extraction from scanned documents and images
Automatic PII detection — SSN, names, emails, phone numbers, IBAN, credit cards, passport numbers, addresses
Multi-regulation support — GDPR, HIPAA, KVKK, CCPA, LGPD, and more
Metadata cleaning — removal of author, creation date, and software information
Batch processing — multiple file redaction with ZIP download (Pro feature)
serviceDesc.text2: All document processing occurs entirely within your web browser. No document content is ever uploaded to, processed by, or stored on our servers.

noGuarantee.heading: 3. Disclaimer — No Guarantee of Complete Redaction
noGuarantee.text: While we strive for the highest accuracy in PII detection, OfflineRedact does not guarantee 100% detection or removal of all sensitive information. Automated pattern matching may miss certain data formats, non-standard patterns, or context-dependent information.
noGuarantee.text2: You are solely responsible for reviewing and verifying all redaction results before sharing, publishing, or distributing any document processed with OfflineRedact. We strongly recommend:
items: Always review the redacted document visually before sharing
Test that redacted text cannot be selected, copied, or extracted
Use manual redaction for any items the automatic detection may have missed
Keep a secure copy of the original document until you have verified the redaction
noGuarantee.text3: OfflineRedact is a tool to assist with document redaction — it does not replace professional legal or compliance review when required.

license.heading: 4. License & Subscription Plans
freeItems: Unlimited single-file PDF, Word, and image redaction
All PII detection patterns and regulation profiles
proItems: Everything in Free — with no watermark
Batch processing — redact multiple files at once
license.text2: Pro subscriptions are billed monthly or annually through Stripe. You may cancel your subscription at any time; your Pro features will remain active until the end of the current billing period.
license.text3: We reserve the right to modify pricing with 30 days' advance notice. Existing subscribers will be grandfathered at their current rate for the remainder of their billing cycle.

refundPolicy.heading: 5. Refund Policy
refundPolicy.text: We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee on all Pro subscriptions. If you are not satisfied with the Service for any reason, you may request a full refund within 30 days of your initial purchase.
items: Refund requests must be submitted to support@offlineredact.com within 30 days of purchase
Refunds are processed to the original payment method within 5-10 business days
After 30 days, no partial or prorated refunds are available
You may cancel your subscription at any time to prevent future charges
refundPolicy.text2: For annual subscriptions, the 30-day guarantee applies from the date of purchase. If you cancel after 30 days, your Pro access continues until the end of the annual period — no refund is issued for the remaining term.

acceptableUse.heading: 6. Acceptable Use Policy
acceptableUse.text: You agree to use OfflineRedact only for lawful purposes. The following uses are prohibited:
prohibited: Using the Service for any illegal activity or to violate any applicable law or regulation
Attempting to reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Service's source code
Automated scraping, crawling, or data extraction from the Service
Attempting to circumvent any access controls, licensing mechanisms, or security measures
Using the Service to process documents you do not have the right to access or modify
Reselling, sublicensing, or redistributing the Service without our written consent
Interfering with or disrupting the Service's infrastructure or other users' access
acceptableUse.text2: Violation of this Acceptable Use Policy may result in immediate termination of your account and access to the Service.

ip.heading: 7. Intellectual Property
ip.text: The OfflineRedact software, website, brand, logos, and all associated content are the intellectual property of OfflineRedact and are protected by copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws.
ip.text2: You retain full ownership of all documents, files, and content that you process using the Service. We claim no rights over your documents. Since all processing happens in your browser, we never have access to your content.
ip.text3: Your Pro license grants you a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable right to use the Service for personal or business purposes in accordance with these Terms.

accountTerms.heading: 8. Account Terms
accountTerms.text: If you create an account with OfflineRedact:
items: You must provide accurate and complete information during registration
You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
You must not share your account or Pro license with other individuals or organizations
You must notify us immediately if you suspect unauthorized access to your account
You may request account deletion at any time by contacting support@offlineredact.com; we will delete your account data within 30 days

availability.heading: 9. Service Availability & Modifications
availability.text: We strive to provide reliable access to OfflineRedact, but we do not guarantee 100% uptime or uninterrupted availability. The Service may be temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, updates, or circumstances beyond our control.
availability.text2: We reserve the right to modify, update, or discontinue any feature of the Service at any time. For material changes that affect Pro subscribers, we will provide at least 30 days' advance notice via email.
availability.text3: Since OfflineRedact's core functionality runs in your browser, the redaction tool works even when offline (after initial page load). This means your workflow is largely unaffected by server availability.

liability.heading: 10. Limitation of Liability
liability.text: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, OFFLINEREDACT AND ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, AND AGENTS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO:
items: Loss of data, revenue, or profits arising from use of the Service
Incomplete or inaccurate redaction of sensitive information
Unauthorized access to or alteration of your account data
Service interruptions or unavailability
Any third-party actions or content
liability.text2: Our total liability for any claims arising from or related to the Service shall not exceed the amount you paid to us in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.
liability.text3: Some jurisdictions do not allow the exclusion of certain warranties or limitation of liability. In such cases, our liability shall be limited to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law.

governingLaw.heading: 11. Governing Law & Jurisdiction
governingLaw.text: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which OfflineRedact is incorporated, without regard to conflict of law provisions.
governingLaw.text2: For users in the European Union, nothing in these Terms affects your rights under mandatory consumer protection laws of your country of residence. Any disputes arising from these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in your country of residence, as provided by applicable EU consumer protection legislation.

disputes.heading: 12. Dispute Resolution
disputes.text: We encourage you to contact us first at legal@offlineredact.com to resolve any disputes informally. Most concerns can be resolved quickly through direct communication.
disputes.text2: If informal resolution is unsuccessful, any dispute shall be resolved through binding arbitration in accordance with the rules of the applicable arbitration institution, rather than in court. You agree to waive your right to participate in class action lawsuits or class-wide arbitration.
disputes.text3: For users in the EU: This arbitration clause does not affect your rights to bring claims before the courts of your country of residence under applicable consumer protection laws. You may also use the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution platform at ec.europa.eu/odr.

severability.heading: 13. Severability & Entire Agreement
severability.text: If any provision of these Terms is found to be unenforceable or invalid by a court of competent jurisdiction, that provision shall be limited or eliminated to the minimum extent necessary so that these Terms shall otherwise remain in full force and effect.
severability.text2: These Terms, together with our Privacy Policy, constitute the entire agreement between you and OfflineRedact regarding the use of the Service, superseding any prior agreements or communications.

contact.heading: 14. Contact Us
contact.text: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service:
items: General support: support@offlineredact.com
Legal inquiries: legal@offlineredact.com
Refund requests: support@offlineredact.com
contact.responseTime: We aim to respond to all inquiries within 2 business days.

cta.title: OfflineRedactを試してみませんか？
cta.subtitle: PDFから機密データの墨消しを開始 — 無料、登録不要。

hipaa-compliant-pdf-redaction.title: HIPAA準拠のPDF墨消し：完全ガイド
hipaa-compliant-pdf-redaction.excerpt: HIPAA準拠を維持するために、PDF文書から保護対象医療情報（PHI）を適切に墨消しする方法を学びます。
content: 医療機関は日々膨大な量の保護対象医療情報（PHI）を取り扱っています。法的手続き、研究、組織間のコミュニケーションなどで外部にドキュメントを共有する際、適切な墨消しは単なるベストプラクティスではなく、HIPAAに基づく法的要件です。
HIPAAのプライバシールールでは、対象事業者とそのビジネスアソシエイトが、すべての個人識別可能な医療情報を保護することを求めています。これには、氏名、日付、社会保障番号、医療記録番号、その他14カテゴリの識別子が含まれます。
単純な視覚的墨消し — 機密テキストに黒いボックスを重ねること — はHIPAA準拠には不十分です。基盤となるテキストデータをドキュメントから完全に削除する必要があります。OfflineRedactは、視覚的に覆うだけでなく、PDFコンテンツストリームから実際のテキストデータを削除する真の墨消しを実行します。
HIPAA準拠の墨消しの主要手順：(1) 自動検出を使用してドキュメント内のすべてのPHIを特定、(2) 検出された項目を確認・承認、(3) テキストデータを削除する真の墨消しを適用、(4) ドキュメントのメタデータをクリーニング、(5) 共有前に墨消し済みドキュメントを検証。
OfflineRedactでは、すべての処理がブラウザ内で行われます — ドキュメントがデバイスから外に出ることはありません。これにより、墨消しプロセス自体でのPHI漏洩リスクが排除されます。これはHIPAA準拠にとって重要な考慮事項です。

ediscovery-document-redaction.title: eDiscoveryドキュメント墨消しのベストプラクティス
ediscovery-document-redaction.excerpt: eDiscoveryプロセスにおける機密情報の墨消しに関する法律専門家向け必須ガイド。
content: 法的手続きにおいて、eDiscoveryプロセスでは大量のドキュメントの提出が求められることがあります。これらのドキュメントの多くには、提出前に墨消しが必要な特権情報、企業秘密、または個人データが含まれています。
eDiscoveryにおける墨消しは複数の目的を果たします：弁護士・依頼者間の秘匿特権の保護、機密ビジネス情報の遮蔽、そして訴訟においても適用される可能性のあるGDPRやCCPAなどのプライバシー規制への準拠。
eDiscovery墨消しにおける一般的な間違いには、真の墨消しの代わりにハイライターツールを使用すること、メタデータの削除を忘れること、ドキュメントセット全体での一貫性のない墨消し、埋め込み画像やフォームフィールドの墨消し漏れなどがあります。
OfflineRedactは一括処理に対応しています — 複数のPDFをアップロードし、一貫した墨消しルールですべてを処理できます。これは、数百から数千のドキュメントに対して均一な処理が必要なeDiscoveryワークフローにとって不可欠です。
ベストプラクティス：(1) 墨消しカテゴリを事前に定義、(2) 一貫性を確保するために自動検出を使用、(3) 必ず第二のレビュアーで検証、(4) 墨消しログを維持、(5) 提出するすべてのドキュメントからメタデータをクリーニング。

gdpr-pdf-compliance.title: GDPR PDF準拠：ドキュメント内の個人データの保護
gdpr-pdf-compliance.excerpt: 適切な墨消しを通じて、PDF文書がGDPRデータ保護要件に準拠していることを確認する方法。
content: 一般データ保護規則（GDPR）は、EU居住者の個人データを保護することを組織に求めています。PDFドキュメントには、データ主体アクセス要求、データ共有契約、ドキュメント保持ポリシーなど、適切に取り扱う必要のある個人データが含まれていることがよくあります。
GDPRにおける個人データには、識別された、または識別可能な個人に関連するあらゆる情報が含まれます：氏名、メールアドレス、電話番号、IPアドレス、財務情報など。PDFドキュメントには、複数のカテゴリの個人データが含まれていることがよくあります。
データ主体アクセス要求（DSAR）に対応する際、組織は第三者の個人データを墨消ししたうえでドキュメントのコピーを提供する必要がある場合があります。これには、他の個人のデータを慎重に特定し、完全に削除することが求められます。
OfflineRedactの複数規制対応には、一般的なヨーロッパの個人データパターン（氏名、メールアドレス、電話番号、IBAN番号、郵便住所）を自動検出するGDPRプロファイルが含まれています。このツールはすべてのEU言語をサポートしています。
PDF墨消しにおけるGDPRの主要考慮事項：(1) 墨消しが不可逆であることを確認 — テキストは完全に削除する必要がある、(2) 個人データを含む可能性のあるドキュメントメタデータをクリーニング、(3) 墨消し活動の記録を維持、(4) データ最小化の原則を適用。

appi-pdf-sumikeshi.title: APPI Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for Japan
appi-pdf-sumikeshi.excerpt: Guide for Japanese organizations on redacting personal information from PDF documents under the Act on Protection of Personal Information.
content: Japan's Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI) was significantly strengthened in its 2022 amendment, imposing stricter requirements on how organizations handle personal data. PDF documents containing personal information must be properly redacted before sharing, archiving, or publishing.

The Personal Information Protection Commission (PPC) enforces APPI compliance and has increased its oversight of digital document handling. Organizations must ensure that redaction of personal information in PDFs is permanent and irreversible — visual masking alone is insufficient.

Japanese-specific personal data patterns include: My Number (Individual Number), Japanese phone numbers (+81 format), Japanese postal codes, bank account numbers, health insurance card numbers, and names in both kanji and katakana formats.

APPI compliance requirements for PDF redaction: (1) Permanent deletion or anonymization of personal information that is no longer needed, (2) Security measures to prevent leakage during processing, (3) Proper handling of sensitive personal information with additional protections, (4) Record-keeping of data handling activities, (5) Notification obligations in case of data breaches.

OfflineRedact processes all documents locally in your browser, with no server uploads required. This architecture inherently supports APPI's security requirements by eliminating data transmission risks. The tool detects Japanese personal information patterns including My Number format and Japanese contact information.

avg-pdf-lakken.title: AVG Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for the Netherlands
avg-pdf-lakken.excerpt: Guide for Dutch organizations on redacting personal data from PDF documents under the Algemene Verordening Gegevensbescherming.
content: The Netherlands implements the EU GDPR as the Algemene Verordening Gegevensbescherming (AVG), enforced by the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (AP). Dutch organizations, particularly in government, healthcare, and financial sectors, must ensure proper PDF redaction when sharing documents containing personal data.

The Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens has been increasingly active in enforcement, with notable fines for improper data handling. For PDF documents, the AP requires that redaction be permanent and irreversible — simple visual overlays that allow text recovery are considered insufficient and constitute a data breach risk.

Dutch-specific personal data patterns include: BSN (Burgerservicenummer / citizen service number), Dutch phone numbers (+31 format), Dutch IBAN numbers (NL prefix), passport and ID card numbers, health insurance numbers, and names following Dutch naming conventions.

AVG compliance requirements for PDF redaction: (1) Permanent removal of personal data from document content, (2) Metadata cleaning including author and creation information, (3) Documentation of redaction activities for accountability, (4) Data protection impact assessment for large-scale redaction projects, (5) Local processing to minimize data exposure risks.

OfflineRedact detects Dutch personal data patterns including BSN format and Dutch contact information. Client-side processing ensures documents stay on your device, supporting the AVG's data minimization and security requirements without the risks of cloud-based processing.

ccpa-pdf-redaction.title: CCPA Compliance: How to Redact Personal Information from PDFs
ccpa-pdf-redaction.excerpt: Guide for California businesses on redacting consumer personal information from PDF documents under CCPA.
content: The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and its amendment, the CPRA, give California consumers the right to know what personal information businesses collect, the right to delete it, and the right to opt out of its sale. When responding to consumer requests or sharing documents, businesses must properly redact personal information from PDFs.

CCPA defines personal information broadly: names, Social Security numbers, email addresses, purchasing history, browsing history, geolocation data, biometric information, and any information that could be linked to a consumer or household. PDF documents in HR, legal, finance, and customer service departments frequently contain multiple categories of this data.

Key CCPA redaction scenarios: (1) Responding to consumer access requests — you may need to share records while redacting third-party information, (2) Data sharing with service providers — contracts and reports may need consumer PII removed, (3) Internal audits — compliance teams reviewing documents may need to work with redacted copies, (4) Litigation holds — legal proceedings may require production of documents with certain PII redacted.

Common CCPA redaction mistakes to avoid: (1) Using visual overlays instead of true redaction — covered text can be extracted, (2) Forgetting to redact metadata containing personal information, (3) Inconsistent redaction across document sets, (4) Uploading documents containing California consumer data to non-compliant cloud services for processing.

OfflineRedact's CCPA regulation profile automatically detects SSNs, ITINs, email addresses, US phone numbers, credit card numbers, and names. The client-side architecture means California consumer data never leaves your device during the redaction process, eliminating the risk of unauthorized disclosure. Batch processing ensures consistent redaction across large document sets.

client-side-vs-server-side-redaction.title: Client-Side vs Server-Side PDF Redaction: Security Comparison
client-side-vs-server-side-redaction.excerpt: Why processing documents in your browser is more secure than uploading them to a server for redaction.
content: When it comes to redacting sensitive information from PDFs, one of the most critical decisions is where the processing happens. Server-side redaction tools require you to upload your documents to a remote server, while client-side tools like OfflineRedact process everything locally in your browser.

Server-side redaction introduces several security risks: (1) Documents are transmitted over the internet, creating interception opportunities, (2) Files are stored on third-party servers, even temporarily, (3) Server breaches can expose all uploaded documents, (4) You must trust the provider's data handling and retention policies, (5) Compliance auditors may flag server-side processing as a risk.

Client-side redaction eliminates these risks entirely. Your documents never leave your device — all PII detection, redaction, and metadata cleaning happens in your browser's JavaScript engine. There is zero network transmission of document content, zero server storage, and zero third-party access to your files.

For regulated industries, this distinction is crucial. HIPAA requires that PHI be protected during processing. GDPR mandates data minimization — why send data to a server when you don't need to? KVKK and CCPA have similar requirements. Client-side processing is inherently more compliant because it removes the server as a potential point of failure.

OfflineRedact uses WebAssembly and browser APIs to achieve the same quality of redaction as server-side tools — automatic PII detection with 13 pattern types, true text removal from PDF content streams, metadata cleaning, and batch processing — all without a single byte of your document data ever leaving your browser.

cnil-rgpd-caviardage-pdf.title: CNIL & RGPD: Complete Guide to PDF Redaction in France
cnil-rgpd-caviardage-pdf.excerpt: How to comply with French data protection requirements when redacting personal data from PDF documents under CNIL guidelines.
content: France's implementation of the GDPR, known locally as the RGPD, is enforced by the CNIL (Commission Nationale de l'Informatique et des Libertés). French organizations must ensure personal data in PDF documents is properly redacted before sharing, particularly when responding to data subject access requests or publishing public records.

The CNIL has issued specific guidance on data anonymization and pseudonymization techniques. For PDF documents, this means that simple visual overlays are insufficient — personal data must be permanently and irreversibly removed from the document's content streams. The CNIL considers recoverable redaction a data breach risk.

French-specific personal data patterns include: INSEE numbers (numéro de sécurité sociale), national identity card numbers, French phone numbers (+33 format), French IBAN numbers (FR prefix), and names following French naming conventions. These must all be detected and properly redacted.

Key RGPD/CNIL compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal data using automated detection with French-specific patterns, (2) Ensure redaction permanently removes text data from the PDF, (3) Clean document metadata including author, creation date, and software information, (4) Maintain redaction logs as required by CNIL accountability obligations, (5) Process documents locally to minimize data exposure.

OfflineRedact supports RGPD compliance with its European regulation profile, automatically detecting French personal data patterns. All processing happens in your browser — no data is sent to external servers, ensuring compliance with CNIL's data minimization principles.

dataskydd-pdf-maskering.title: Swedish Data Protection: PDF Redaction Guide
dataskydd-pdf-maskering.excerpt: Guide for Swedish organizations on redacting personal data from PDF documents in compliance with dataskyddsförordningen and IMY guidelines.
content: Sweden implements the EU GDPR through the dataskyddsförordningen, enforced by Integritetsskyddsmyndigheten (IMY). Swedish organizations, including public authorities subject to the principle of public access (offentlighetsprincipen), must properly redact personal data from PDF documents before disclosure or sharing.

IMY has issued guidance on proper anonymization and pseudonymization of personal data in documents. Swedish organizations face the unique challenge of balancing the constitutional principle of public access with GDPR data protection requirements — making proper PDF redaction essential when releasing public documents.

Swedish-specific personal data patterns include: personnummer (personal identity number), samordningsnummer (coordination number), Swedish phone numbers (+46 format), Swedish IBAN numbers (SE prefix), and names following Swedish naming conventions.

Swedish data protection compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal data including Swedish-specific identifiers, (2) Apply permanent redaction that removes text from document content streams, (3) Clean document metadata, (4) Balance public access requirements with data protection obligations, (5) Maintain redaction logs for accountability.

OfflineRedact processes all documents locally in your browser, supporting IMY's data protection requirements. The tool detects Swedish personal data patterns including personnummer and Swedish contact formats, helping organizations comply with both dataskyddsförordningen and the principle of public access.

dsgvo-pdf-schwaerzung.title: DSGVO-Compliant PDF Redaction: Complete Guide
dsgvo-pdf-schwaerzung.excerpt: Learn how to redact personal data from PDF documents in compliance with Germany's DSGVO (GDPR) requirements and permanently remove it.
content: The General Data Protection Regulation (DSGVO in German) imposes strict requirements on handling personal data in the EU. PDF documents frequently contain sensitive information such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, IBAN numbers, and addresses that must be protected before sharing.

Article 17 of the DSGVO — the right to erasure — requires organizations to permanently remove personal data on request. For PDF documents, this means irreversible redaction, not just visual covering. The underlying text data must be completely removed from the document.

Common PDF redaction mistakes: (1) Using highlighters or black bars that can be copied and pasted, (2) Forgetting metadata cleanup (author, software, creation date), (3) Inconsistent redaction across multiple documents, (4) Uploading sensitive documents to cloud-based services.

OfflineRedact solves these problems through true redaction directly in your browser. No server upload required — your documents never leave your device. The DSGVO profile automatically detects European personal data: names, email addresses, phone numbers, IBAN numbers, and postal addresses in all 24 EU languages.

Best practices for DSGVO-compliant PDF redaction: (1) Use automated detection for consistent results, (2) Ensure redaction is irreversible, (3) Clean document metadata, (4) Keep records of redaction activities per Art. 5(2) DSGVO accountability, (5) Process documents locally to minimize data breach risk.

gdpr-italia-oscuramento-pdf.title: GDPR Italy: Complete Guide to PDF Redaction
gdpr-italia-oscuramento-pdf.excerpt: How Italian organizations can ensure GDPR compliance through proper PDF redaction under Garante per la protezione dei dati personali guidelines.
content: Italy's implementation of the GDPR is overseen by the Garante per la protezione dei dati personali, one of Europe's most active data protection authorities. Italian organizations must ensure PDF documents are properly redacted before sharing, particularly in contexts involving public administration, healthcare, and judicial proceedings.

The Garante has issued specific provisions on document anonymization and has levied significant fines for improper data handling. Italian organizations must go beyond visual masking — personal data must be permanently removed from PDF content streams to be considered properly redacted.

Italian-specific personal data patterns include: Codice Fiscale (tax identification code), Carta d'Identità numbers, Italian phone numbers (+39 format), Italian IBAN numbers (IT prefix), health card numbers (tessera sanitaria), and names following Italian naming conventions.

Italian GDPR compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal data including Italian-specific identifiers, (2) Apply irreversible redaction to document content streams, (3) Clean metadata from all shared documents, (4) Maintain processing records as required by the Garante, (5) Ensure data minimization in document handling workflows.

OfflineRedact supports Italian GDPR compliance with its European regulation profile detecting Codice Fiscale, Italian phone formats, and IBAN numbers. All processing happens locally in your browser, ensuring no personal data is transmitted to external servers.

kvkk-pdf-redaction-guide.title: KVKK Compliant PDF Redaction: Comprehensive Guide
kvkk-pdf-redaction-guide.excerpt: Learn how to properly redact personal data in PDF documents in compliance with Turkey's KVKK regulation.
content: Turkey's Personal Data Protection Law (KVKK), enacted in 2016, imposes strict rules on how organizations process and share personal data. PDF documents frequently contain sensitive data such as TC identity numbers, phone numbers, email addresses, and IBAN numbers.

Article 7 of KVKK mandates the deletion, destruction, or anonymization of personal data when the reasons requiring its processing no longer exist. The most effective way to fulfill this obligation in PDF documents is to apply permanent redaction.

Visual masking — drawing black boxes over sensitive text — is not sufficient for KVKK compliance. The underlying text data must be permanently removed from the document. OfflineRedact performs true redaction by removing actual text data from PDF content streams.

OfflineRedact's KVKK profile automatically detects Turkey-specific personal data patterns: TC Identity numbers (with checksum validation), Turkish phone numbers (+90 format), email addresses, IBAN numbers, names, and addresses. All processing happens in your browser — your documents never leave your device.

Steps for KVKK-compliant PDF redaction: (1) Detect all personal data with automatic detection, (2) Review and confirm detected items, (3) Apply true redaction that removes text data, (4) Clean document metadata (author, creation date, etc.), (5) Verify the redacted document before sharing.

lgpd-redacao-pdf.title: LGPD Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for Brazil
lgpd-redacao-pdf.excerpt: How Brazilian organizations can ensure LGPD compliance when redacting personal data from PDF documents.
content: Brazil's Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) establishes comprehensive data protection requirements similar to the EU's GDPR. Organizations operating in Brazil must properly redact personal data from PDF documents when sharing externally, responding to data subject requests, or complying with data minimization obligations.

The Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados (ANPD) oversees LGPD enforcement and has increasingly focused on digital document handling practices. Improper PDF redaction — where personal data can be recovered from supposedly redacted documents — constitutes a violation of LGPD's data security requirements.

Brazilian-specific personal data patterns include: CPF numbers (Cadastro de Pessoas Físicas), CNPJ numbers (company registration), Brazilian phone numbers (+55 format), Brazilian bank account details, RG numbers (identity card), and names following Brazilian naming conventions.

LGPD compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal data including Brazilian-specific identifiers using automated detection, (2) Apply true redaction that removes text data from PDF content streams, (3) Clean document metadata that may contain personal information, (4) Maintain records of processing activities as required by Article 37, (5) Ensure local processing to comply with data transfer restrictions.

OfflineRedact supports LGPD compliance with its Latin American regulation profile, detecting CPF numbers, Brazilian phone formats, and other local identifiers. All processing happens entirely in your browser, ensuring no personal data is transmitted to external servers.

lopdgdd-redaccion-pdf.title: LOPDGDD Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for Spain
lopdgdd-redaccion-pdf.excerpt: Guide for Spanish organizations on redacting personal data from PDF documents under LOPDGDD and EU GDPR requirements.
content: Spain's Ley Orgánica de Protección de Datos Personales y Garantía de los Derechos Digitales (LOPDGDD) complements the EU GDPR with additional national provisions. Spanish organizations handling PDF documents containing personal data must ensure proper redaction to comply with both LOPDGDD and GDPR requirements.

The Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) actively enforces data protection violations and has issued significant fines for improper data handling. Inadequate PDF redaction — such as using visual overlays that can be removed — is considered a data protection failure under LOPDGDD.

Spanish-specific personal data to detect includes: DNI/NIE numbers, Spanish phone numbers (+34 format), Spanish IBAN numbers (ES prefix), Social Security numbers, and names following Spanish naming conventions with two surnames.

LOPDGDD compliance requirements for PDF redaction: (1) Permanent removal of personal data from document content streams, (2) Metadata cleaning to remove hidden personal information, (3) Documentation of redaction activities for accountability under Article 5(2) GDPR, (4) Implementation of privacy by design principles, (5) Local processing to minimize data transfer risks.

OfflineRedact's European regulation profile automatically detects Spanish personal data patterns including DNI/NIE numbers, Spanish phone formats, and IBAN numbers. Client-side processing ensures documents never leave your device, meeting LOPDGDD's strict data protection requirements.

pdf-redaction-healthcare-checklist.title: PDF Redaction for Healthcare: HIPAA Compliance Checklist
pdf-redaction-healthcare-checklist.excerpt: A practical checklist for healthcare organizations to ensure HIPAA-compliant PDF redaction workflows.
content: Healthcare organizations face unique challenges when sharing documents externally. Patient records, insurance forms, lab results, and clinical notes all contain Protected Health Information (PHI) that must be redacted before disclosure. This checklist ensures your PDF redaction workflow meets HIPAA requirements.

PHI Categories to Redact: HIPAA defines 18 PHI identifiers that must be protected: (1) Names, (2) Geographic data smaller than a state, (3) Dates related to an individual, (4) Phone numbers, (5) Fax numbers, (6) Email addresses, (7) Social Security numbers, (8) Medical record numbers, (9) Health plan beneficiary numbers, (10) Account numbers, (11) Certificate/license numbers, (12) Vehicle identifiers, (13) Device identifiers, (14) Web URLs, (15) IP addresses, (16) Biometric identifiers, (17) Full-face photos, (18) Any other unique identifying number.

Pre-Redaction Checklist: Before redacting, verify that (1) you have identified all PHI in the document, (2) you are using a tool that performs true redaction (text removal, not visual overlay), (3) the tool cleans document metadata, (4) the tool processes documents locally or in a HIPAA-compliant environment, (5) you have a process for quality review after redaction.

Post-Redaction Verification: After redacting, confirm that (1) redacted text cannot be recovered by selecting, copying, or using text extraction tools, (2) document metadata has been cleaned (author name, creation software, timestamps), (3) the redaction log has been saved for compliance records, (4) the original unredacted document is stored securely or destroyed per retention policy.

OfflineRedact streamlines this entire workflow with its HIPAA regulation profile. It automatically detects SSNs, names, phone numbers, email addresses, and other PHI identifiers. All processing happens in your browser — no PHI is ever transmitted to external servers, making it inherently HIPAA-compliant for the redaction process itself.

pipa-pdf-masking.title: PIPA Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for South Korea
pipa-pdf-masking.excerpt: How Korean organizations can comply with PIPA requirements when redacting personal information from PDF documents.
content: South Korea's Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA) is one of the strictest data protection laws in Asia. Organizations must implement rigorous measures to protect personal information in PDF documents, including proper redaction before external sharing or when responding to data subject access requests.

The Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) actively enforces PIPA violations with substantial penalties. Improper PDF redaction — where personal information remains recoverable — can result in administrative fines and criminal penalties under PIPA's enforcement provisions.

Korean-specific personal data patterns include: Resident Registration Numbers, Korean phone numbers (+82 format), Korean passport numbers, driver's license numbers, health insurance numbers, and names in Hangul and romanized formats.

PIPA compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal information using automated detection with Korean-specific patterns, (2) Apply permanent redaction that removes data from document content streams, (3) Destroy or anonymize personal information when the purpose of processing has been achieved, (4) Implement technical safeguards as required by PIPA Article 29, (5) Maintain processing records for regulatory audits.

OfflineRedact's client-side architecture aligns with PIPA's strict data security requirements — all document processing happens in your browser with zero server transmission. The tool supports Korean personal information patterns including Resident Registration Number detection and Korean contact formats.

pipl-pdf-tumin.title: PIPL Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for China
pipl-pdf-tumin.excerpt: Guide for organizations operating in China on redacting personal information from PDF documents under the Personal Information Protection Law.
content: China's Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL), effective since November 2021, establishes comprehensive personal information protection requirements. Organizations handling PDF documents containing personal information of Chinese residents must ensure proper redaction to comply with PIPL's strict data handling obligations.

The Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC) and other regulatory bodies enforce PIPL compliance with significant penalties including fines up to 50 million yuan or 5% of annual revenue. Improper handling of personal information in documents, including inadequate redaction, can trigger enforcement actions.

Chinese-specific personal data patterns include: Resident Identity Card numbers, Chinese phone numbers (+86 format), Chinese bank account numbers, social insurance numbers, passport numbers, and names in simplified Chinese characters.

PIPL compliance requirements for PDF redaction: (1) Process personal information only for clear and reasonable purposes, (2) Apply permanent redaction when information is no longer needed for its original purpose, (3) Implement technical security measures to prevent unauthorized access, (4) Minimize personal information collection and processing, (5) Conduct personal information protection impact assessments for sensitive data.

OfflineRedact's fully client-side processing architecture is particularly relevant for PIPL compliance, as it eliminates cross-border data transfer concerns — documents never leave the user's device. The tool supports detection of Chinese personal information patterns including ID card numbers and Chinese contact formats.

rodo-redakcja-pdf.title: RODO Compliance: PDF Redaction Guide for Poland
rodo-redakcja-pdf.excerpt: How Polish organizations can comply with RODO (GDPR) requirements when redacting personal data from PDF documents.
content: Poland implements the EU GDPR as RODO (Rozporządzenie o Ochronie Danych Osobowych), enforced by the Urząd Ochrony Danych Osobowych (UODO). Polish organizations must ensure personal data in PDF documents is permanently redacted before sharing, particularly in administrative, legal, and healthcare contexts.

UODO has actively enforced data protection requirements, issuing fines for inadequate personal data protection. For PDF documents, this means organizations cannot rely on visual redaction methods — personal data must be permanently removed from the document's underlying text content to comply with RODO requirements.

Polish-specific personal data patterns include: PESEL numbers (national identification), NIP numbers (tax identification), REGON numbers (business registration), Polish phone numbers (+48 format), Polish IBAN numbers (PL prefix), and names following Polish naming conventions with diacritical characters.

RODO compliance steps for PDF redaction: (1) Identify all personal data using automated detection with Polish-specific patterns, (2) Apply true redaction removing data from PDF content streams, (3) Clean document metadata, (4) Maintain records of processing activities as required by RODO, (5) Process documents locally to minimize data transfer and exposure risks.

OfflineRedact supports RODO compliance by detecting Polish personal data patterns including PESEL and NIP numbers. All processing happens in your browser — no personal data is transmitted to servers, fully supporting UODO's requirements for data security and minimization.
